plan: Await and review the results of the right hand/arm X-ray.

judge_verdict.reasoning: All tools that ran successfully are broadly consistent, but none provide a definitive answer. The image_analysis tool returned empty findings (no detectable pathology). The image_explainability tool ran but produced extremely low confidence scores across all conditions (all sigmoid scores <0.001), with the image detected as 'other' rather than a standard X-ray modality — meaning it could not reliably classify the image. The text_reasoning tool correctly noted that without interpretable imaging results, it cannot confirm or deny a fracture, and assigned a very low confidence of 0.2. The synthesis appropriately combined these weak signals into a clinically reasonable narrative, noting that the mechanism supports fracture suspicion but automated tools could not confirm it. There is no genuine clinical disagreement between tools — they all essentially say 'inconclusive/unable to determine.' This is consensus on uncertainty, not conflict. However, overall confidence is low because the core clinical question (confirm fracture and type) remains unanswered by the AI tools.
